reviews.review_text:
I still come back to this game from time to time since its early access days. Back then it was full of bugs, but somehow my friend and I still had a great time playing it. The difference between the old version and the current one is huge. The game kept getting updates, new content, and improvements over the years.

Cut trees, gather stone, survive the weather, build your base, upgrade your gear, capture thralls, clear dungeons, kill bosses, and just try to stay alive in this massive world. Conan really nails that survival sandbox feeling for me.

The new UE5 update made the game look much better visually and honestly it’s the reason I started playing again after years. I also really like how they connected Exiled Lands and Siptah together. Being able to teleport between the maps feels like a smart change instead of splitting the content apart.

My favorite part of the game is definitely the follower/thrall system. Hunting for strong thralls and traveling around the world with them makes the world feel more alive and gives me a reason to keep exploring. The mod support is also great. There are so many amazing mods, especially for solo play, and I use quite a few of them myself.

The game still has some issues here and there, and the biggest problem right now is probably the servers. Busy servers can get extremely laggy and they still haven’t fully solved that problem. But even with its flaws, Conan is still one of my favorite open world survival games.
fun game but servers are absolute dog **** and ping is insane ever after all these years funcom is unable to give players a good foundation to play on

THE PING IN THE Servers are sooo bad you should have focused on stability first ive played this game on console for year i can understand some ping issues on there but on pc is just inexcusable at this time i can not say its worth buying at this time yes do it look amazing but that dont help when the thing you are fighting is ghost hitting you....

I've played a lot of survival games over the years, but very few truly capture the feeling of surviving the way Conan Exiles does. With over 300 hours invested into the game, I can honestly say Conan Exiles is an experience every survival fan should try at least once. The immersion, brutal combat, atmosphere, and constant fight to survive in the unforgiving world of the Exiled Lands creates something that still feels unique even after all these years.

With the release of Conan Exiles Enhanced on Unreal Engine 5, the game looks absolutely breathtaking. The lighting, environmental detail, shadows, and overall visual fidelity breathe new life into the world. Funcom and Inflexion Games did an incredible job modernizing the experience while still preserving the soul of the original game.

That said, even after 8 years, there are still features and improvements I would love to see added. First and foremost, optimization still needs work. I’m running the game on a high-end PC, and there are moments where performance noticeably struggles. Even certain enemy animations, like the Colossal Snake, can feel laggy or stutter during encounters. With Unreal Engine 5 now powering the game, smoother optimization should absolutely be a priority moving forward.

One feature I would absolutely love is a Ships & Boats DLC. Conan Exiles has massive rivers, coastlines, and oceans that feel underutilized. Imagine starting with small rafts early game, then progressing toward larger customizable vessels that function almost like mobile bases. Being able to craft, store items, place beds as spawn points, and traverse the waters of the Exiled Lands would completely transform exploration and roleplay. Similar to mounts, but restricted to waterways and oceans. The potential there is massive.

I’d also love to see Thralls expanded further. Thralls are one of Conan Exiles’ most unique mechanics and honestly one of the biggest reasons the game stands apart from other survival titles. More animations, interactions, routines, and functionality would make bases feel far more alive and immersive. Watching Thralls train, eat, converse, patrol dynamically, or react to world events would add so much personality to settlements.

Another major improvement would be making the world itself feel more dynamic and unpredictable again. I understand the Purge system was considered too difficult in the past, but I would love the option to enable truly random Purges for players who want that constant tension. The Exiled Lands are brutal by design, but currently the world can sometimes feel too static. Adding dynamic world events like famines, plagues, festivals, ambushes, roaming warbands, or biome-specific disasters would make survival feel far more intense and memorable.

The weather system especially has enormous potential now that the game runs on Unreal Engine 5. Imagine blizzards in the North that drastically reduce visibility and temperature, brutal heat waves in the desert alongside the Maelstrom, monsoons in the jungle, hurricanes along coastal regions, and violent thunderstorms that make exploration genuinely dangerous. Make the North feel freezing cold again. Make the world itself feel hostile and alive.

Despite its flaws, Conan Exiles remains one of the most immersive and ambitious survival games ever made. The foundation is already incredible. With additional optimizations, bug fixes, expanded world systems, and more dynamic gameplay features, this game could easily evolve from a 9/10 into a true 10/10 survival masterpiece.

I have more than 2,688 hours in the game since it started. Hackers and exploiters have been present since the beginning. Lately I have found the situation has become worse and my actual character was hacked during a hack raid some months ago and all knowledge points deleted. The character is no longer usable for most of the content. I have been raising tickets but no one gets back to you or helps fix it. Now I find you can no longer even submit tickets for support.

The game has been great to play over the years despite everything, but in it's current state with no support I can no longer recommend it.

I can't recommend this game to anyone really, which is a shame because so many aspects of this game are fantastic.

The reason the game is bad and I regret ever spending money and time on it is because how some of the content is sold in the game shop.

Basically there are buildings, armors, weapons etc that are only available for a certain period of time and then they become unavailable. They call it market rotation. They may become available again, but no one knows when, maybe never. This is an extreme example of FOMO manipulation with the players (FOMO = Fear of Missing Out).

I am very disappointed because I was enjoying the game and I had these great plans about how I am going to build my future lore-accurate characters and settlements based on the cool buildings and armors that I saw were in the game, but then finding out that they are not available for sale. They are in the game and in the game files, but they are just not in the shop and possibly won't be available.

It is not just a few specific things, it is A LOT of content that is just not available. Google "Conan Exiles Black Lotus Bazaar" and see pages and pages of items and buildings that just can't be acquired anymore.

So screw you Funcom, I wanted to spend more time (and money) on your game, but I won't because you chose to go the full FOMO route and it was not really clear from the start.

With over 4k hours playing this game the new update to the graphics are amazing but it’s immediately overshadowed by the horrible lag… the servers cannot handle more than 10 players without you teleporting all over the place.

Fix your servers and this game would be top 10 played on steam or any console. 

Another issue all the banned accounts became unbanned with the update and a lot of players returned and todays update they are all banned again… good move..

Pets, Followers, Crafting, Building , Exploring, Fighting, riding, swimming. 
Its got it all

If you like open world exploration and liked Skyrim, im sure this would wet your pallet too.
Controller friendly and can be played single player or co-op too.

Many bosses and dungeons to explore, this game should last you a long time.

This is a thumbs down review, but the game has many positives and negatives at the same time. For me, the negatives are more. For context, I play PvE coop, I don't have random people on my server, I don't care for PvP and I play without mods.

The positives are:
- atmosphere
- a good amount of crafting and gathering systems and content
- level design and environment - this team exceeded all of their colleagues here

The negatives:
 - the game design systems are poor both in design and balance, inconsistent and not well connected to other gameplay elements, worst of all - exploration; many things you get when you already don't need them, looting undermines crafting, magic is basically a joke besides portals - some spells I couldn't get benefit from even once; some systems are way too much work for their effect or feel like an unfinished thought; progression of enemies in different zones and even boss locations felt too horizontal (partly because AI is so bad that once you get the game they can hardly hit you, so numbers doesn't matter).
 - the world often felt like a movie set with no movie made - you go to places to find 95% nothing you need or that excites you; great scenery with nothing in it and 3-hit-kill imitation of a boss with no significant special moves;
 - every piece of lore and content is so hidden, that basically I found none the first 50 hours; same goes for mechanics; for me this is "Chatting with ChatGPT - the game" - since I don't have 1000 hours to spend finding what is done how and where is it possible to find a piece of the puzzle, if you carry item X, do thing Y after you've unlocked Z. If I hadn't googled those, so we can get some interesting experience, my score would be 3/10 instead of 5/10 and no, I am not a player that always needs to be spoon-fed and follow a quest marker, but there is exploration and there is "a jackpot to find the slightest glimpse of story or goal". Go the the east end of a map randomly, kill a boss while it is permanently stunned in coop, get an item, go to the other end of the map, find a door in a village with 100 houses that is slightly open, randomly decide to press the interact button. Are you serious guys?
 - on a technical level the game is very bad, AI is extremely poor, physics are clunky, bosses get stuck in trees or a rock, then fly up in space, we got teleported within a decoration zone you are never supposed to reach, within some kind of a dragon, outside the map and with invisible colliders to prevent us go back in the level and had to use admin commands to get out, NPCs are randomly teleported in places that don't make sense, weight not properly indicated and many many many others; even admin commands are buggy...
 - unskippable intro you need to rename a file to skip and no, it is not loading time cover-up (the game loads for 5 seconds now) and yes, if it was, I would prefer watching a progress bar on a black screen instead of the same animation 50 times
 - UI/UX is inconsistent and very clunky, one of the worst I've seen; you can't even use the same split stack mechanics in a storage box as you do in inventory; some things you craft one way with recipes, others magically auto-craft without you knowing what to put, some items are not upgradable with no apparent reason why or message and many many others; you have no VFX on important objects like unlocking recipes which can be basically every decoration item. Seriously, it is 10 times harder to find what you made clickable in the hundreth cave than defeat the guards. Is the actual fun really in pixel-hunting around dark rooms and not reaching and fighting through the place?

Overall your experience with this game can vary greatly, but for me it was rather empty, although I think it had good potential that could have been easily realized with better decisions of the team, some more proficiency and work.

It is still better than what Dune Awakening offers at its early stages.
Sorry for the criticism, colleagues. Good luck with your next projects.

Best UE4 --> UE5 ever made. Why?
1. Did not break the game on Linux
2. Runs alot better than the UE4 version 
3. No bugs spotted so far 
4. Looks AMAZING
The Guys put the standard of how to move a game from UE4 to UE5

Yeah, the UE5 upgrade is great and the game has loads of potential but the ping on every server makes the game unplayable. When the game is working there are times that your hits just don't connect. Climbing is awful in the game. If you want to try and avoid the terrible ping issue and play coop, you have to deal with tethering where you're teleported back to the host if you get too far away. I can't recommend the game because Funcom has had 8 years to polish and refine this game and they've just fumbled the bag. It's honestly sad, this game and Dune could be a money printing machine for them if they would just get them up to snuff.

Came back after many years to enjoy the UE5 update.
Sadly the servers are very laggy, everything is rubberbanding - and on my server I have 300 plus ping on my 1000/1000 connection - so I can't join & play with my character.
I will avoid the game for now.

I'm really trying to like this game.  I've started 6 different characters on 6 different Official servers and can never find the server again even when I make it a favorite.  And when one finally shows up its Ping is too high it wont let me start. You cannot tell if a server is on the East, West or Central US.  I want to play, but there are only so many times I want to start over.  Otherwise it looks great and I find it enjoyable.  I would wait until they figure out the ping and server details to purchase.

Conan Exiles Enhanced is actually really good, and I’d recommend it if the official servers weren’t plagued with the ping lock issue.

I’m on the west coast playing on a west coast official server, and my ping will randomly jump from around 90 to 350+ for no reason. The problem isn’t server lag, the server ping locks you out completely. You literally can’t log in and play once it spikes high enough.

This isn’t an issue with my internet. Every other online game works perfectly fine. It’s specifically Conan official servers doing this.

That’s what makes it frustrating. The actual Enhanced experience is solid and there’s clearly a lot of effort put into it. But it doesn’t matter how good the content is if players can’t even access the server because of an unexplained ping restriction.

Fix the ping lock/server connection issue and this would easily be a positive review.

The greed is too much. The DLC was fine, then they came out with a battlepass and a in game store. Then they realized people were farming crom coins for free from the battle pass, so they removed that, now they just have the DLCs and the bazaar where all the new items go to at exorbitant prices..........

Even this level of greed would be tolerable, IF ONLY they fixed their game. Same bugs from 7 years ago are still there in the game. Single player is torture without mods because simple game mechanics don't work. 
The game itself is great but there are just too many gamebreaking bugs, which were remedied by mods, but now with the UE5 update, those mods don't work anymore.

The official servers are the worst of any survival game. First they will be down for 18 hours with no work from funcom at all. Then you cant join a server over 300 ping. Only problem is one say the ping is 20ms the next it is 599ms. You can refresh the server list but the ping wont change unless you relaunch the game. This now brings me to the unskipable cut scene on start up... There are post for all this going back 3 + years and still none of it fixed.
The actually game is fantastic, however, the online experience is atrocious for the enhanced version.

That was such a disappointing experience.
Conan Exiles start very well, it's a pretty game, the world is large, unique and good looking, and combat seems interesting, at first.
But that's kinda where the fun ends, because almost every mechanic is ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up, boring, or simply poorly thought of, rendering Conan Exiles 10x more painful and annoying than it is pleasant to play.


[b]Combat[/b]
- You'll almost never have a "close fight". Enemies either die or butcher you in a couple of hits. Because that's the main problem with this game: Armor is worthless, while damage is extremely high. I upgraded to armor pieces worth 200+ armors and still feel as defenseless as when fully naked. I can't count the amount of times where a sneaky "medecine man" slaughtered my entire health bar in the blink of an eye, even though I was in an area way beneath my level.
- Add to that the "Hyper armor hell". You get used to being able to stun enemies, for them to suddenly become immune to any form of stun because they triggered one specific move.
- Plus how the game "retains inputs", doesn't let you cancel an attack with a roll (in some cases you can, but when exactly is still unclear to me), so if you are about to hit an enemy who triggers hyper armor, you may have no choice but to die because you can't dodge. 
- Bonus ♥♥♥♥♥♥: Arrows deal insane amounts of damage. I still have grade I archers in the starting area completely rip me apart even though I have armor far beyond their tech level, wtf. 
- And icing on the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥: This game has one of the worst stamina bars I’ve seen, and I’ve played Dark Souls, Elden Ring, and a bunch more. It takes FOREVER to recharge, while dodging costs way more  stamina than it ever should… 
All in all it renders combat very boring, you have to play it veeery safe. Trying to get in the melee and reacting to incoming moves will get you killed due to being out of stamina basically all the time (in an even fight)

[b]Construction[/b]: 
- Awfully hard to snap the way you want, particularly with triangular pieces.
- Triangular pieces were a good idea that allows for cool looking builds (hexagonal or triangular), but are insanely unpractical: Almost every crafting station is block-ish, wasting so much space in a building with such shapes ; And they are a massive pain to build floors with: Stairs always end up misaligned, no way to put a ladder without that ridiculous looking "ladder door" that can only be a square, triangles don't snap to angle pillars (the only relevant pillars)
-> And this begs the question, where is the simple 1/2 foundation (a right triangle, e.g square cut diagonally in half) that every other survival game has? From a construction liberty's perspective, not having a right triangle foundation is far more detrimental than having these ridiculous tiny triangles.

[b]PNJs[/b]:
- Pretty annoying companion controls and behaviors, they keep blocking you, keep having to move them away… Even bugthesda's Skyrim companions move out of the way, not here... 
- You can only tell them to attack targets when said targer is already way too close, and already aggroed onto you.
- They remain where you died,so you best not die to a boss or some isolated location, or your companions will just get killed while you make your way back. 

[b]Crafting[/b]:
- You need such a stupid amount of resources to craft anything, that playing Conan Exiles often feels more work than fun. Even doubling resource acquisition still feels too little. And to top that, devs didn’t think to give us a slider to increase inventory storage, so it’s always a pain regardless of the loot drop setting you picked.
- The tech tree is… bad, like «wow» levels of bad. I know they’ve made improvements, but it’s still unreadable, and I’m so exhausted with the DLC content clutter and that button «hide/show DLC content» that doesn’t do a thing… 
- Tons of areas require thermal protection but so far I haven’t seen any info about thermal protection on any gear I have access to, which is a lot (lvl 50+)
- On the note of having no clear instructions, this game doesn’t provide any besides the most bare tutorial. You’ll never know clearly how to progress forward, half of the crafting station don’t give you their recipes, 80% of items feel useless because no info about usage is provided.
And "Journey" paths... really are just lazy checklists with forgettable rewards and a lack of coherence, so a lack of learning.
- Soooo many resources... particularly flowers, did we really need so many flowers? 
Adding to that, storage is a pain. You can only craft from storage from a ridiculously small distance, while youneed a whole castle full of chests to sort things "cleanly"... Or as clean as you can when the game makes it so hard to have a clean storage (very limited in where you can place signposts to indicate storage content, chest name only appears when you are so close that you already see the content)

[b]Exploration[/b]:
The game's world is really fascinating, but with how difficulty progression is managed, it made exploration feel... bland. It's hard to explain, so I'll just narrate my journey:
- Starting south of the river, everything North and some distance East beats my ass effortlessly, because I need Iron to make decent gear. 
- I eventually build in a better spot, get loads of iron, so I can finally flex my strength against those spiders, rock creatures, and... well literally everything in the desert (including mammoths, skeletons, bandit camps), half of the jungle and the Highlands (even part of the tundra was fine).
So basically, I was stuck in a tiny bit of land, and as soon as I unlocked good iron input, ~70% of the map became my playground. It felt like there was hardly any difficulty progression throughout so much of my exploration.
- So I end up just wandering around, trying to find "the next thing", but everywhere is just iron and coal, the same enemies. Cool structures and landscapes sure, but just the same, homogenized difficulty, over such a huge piece of land. I was so disappointed going into the Jungle where I just murdered anything in 3 hits and effortlessly slapped the witch queen down. 
- Then I hit the volcano, and all enemies are a solid 5x stronger than anything anywhere else, and for the first time I'm taking environmental damage. 
It feels like devs forgot about difficulty ramp up, only to add it all at the very end of the game. 

[b]Other annoyances[/b]:
- Can’t remove an equiped item from the hotbar, gotta first unequip it manually.
- Most consumables grant bonuses or maluses that you have no way of guessing ahead of time.
- Full of tiny bugs, like right now I got a "reduced stamina regen" but it actually completely stopped regenerating... So having fun doing nothing for 5 whole minutes, cool design devs. 
- And much larger bugs, such as the one I just got that leads me to posting this review before uninstalling the game, that vanished my absolute best thrall equiped with the few legendary items I had acquired. His icon is on the map, I tried flying to the roof and even ghosting through the floor, teleport away and back... but he's just not here. 

Talk about a 8 years old game with recent updates... Honestly, crap experience, even Return to the Moria feels better developped than this trash of a game.

Thanks Funcom for a free re-work........the game is literally unplayable due to rubber banding and lag that will make you wish these developers and ARK developers got their S*** together.  Can't do it for now.  Patch the game and make it playable PLEASE.

Fun game but servers are absolute dog **** and ping is insane. Great when the servers are under maybe 20 pop. Horrible above that. And can't refresh server list until game finds all servers which takes 10 min. or restarting game. when 20+ people are on the server have to restart game for 50 times before i can get under 300 ping on it to join. It says server is for 40 people and can't handle even half of it.

I have given this game a couple of chances, years ago server instability caused me to stop my playthrough.
Recently tried again and played a solid few hours on an official server, now I haven't been able to log on to the same server for the last 2 days due to the game telling me my ping will be too high to join the server...
No more tries
Das Update hat die Performance so gewaltig in den Boden gerissen. Auch geschenkt würd ichs aktuell nicht empfehlen.
Couldnt get into to game now that its Enhanced I cant get off lol. The mods in workshop make it extra fun.

[h1] A good game they don't want you to play with friends. A bad game as a result. [/h1]

Windrose made this game's spiritual successor without a major corporation involved and they allow you to invite your friends and play without the massive restrictions this game forces down your unwilling throats. And those of us unable to port forward are forced to use specific vendors charging $20/month after tax just to have fun with others off the toxic public servers.

No matter how good you make the game or update it, it will fail like Dune. The major reason is your barrier to entry for people to play this with others and have a reliably enjoyable time. Good job on the update, bad job on the ability to play with others.

It's a really solid game, assuming you are into this brutal, low fantasy depiction of times pre-antiquity, that could be best summed up with the general perception of Conan's personal god, Crom:
'Crom breathes the will to strive into Cimmerian at birth and then answers no prayers. Cimmerians don't pray to him. In fact, they believe that if you call his name, he’s more likely to send a disaster your way just for being a "whining weakling."'.

It is buggy in some places, and full of typical Funcom experience. Nonetheless, with its setting and adequate maturity that doesn't shy from the setting's brutality, it is survival game done right, that does capture the survival feeling just right, with interesting locations, NPC, progression, combat and background lore.

And the recent UE5 upgrade is chef's kiss, with Frame Gen and DLSS quality i manage to run it on ultra settings, in 4k, in stable, comfortable, 70-110 FPS, so thank you, Funcom, really, for possibly giving the game second breath in 2026, a game that i was returning to every now and then regardless.
Really Really wanted to enjoy this one the gameplay is good the graphics are amazing, but the servers are pure lag, only way to get around this is rent a server or play single player

-=(May 2026)=-
The game, itself is amazing; tons of fun to play. But all of that is undone by a woefully bad online experience.

The blame lies squarely on GPortal, which (as of this review) hosts the Official servers. It's clearly not the game engine as both Dune Awakening and Conan Exiles run UE5 (as do other online games with similar 'per world' player counts). None of those other games experience this level of lag.

As such, I cannot in good conscious recommend a game that is marketed as a 'multiplayer game' but can only be played to a satisfactory level in 'singleplayer'.

To Funcom: I highly recommend you break the service contract with GPortal. Their lackluster service provided to you is costing you money.

This game was amazing when first came out and you realized mods were possible.
Was amazing when they added all those DLC that were reasonable priced and added lots of stuff to the game.
This game was great when they added a second, open world, map FOR FREE
This game was amazing when they fixed the bugs and ironed it to a perfect shape

now, with the UE5 FOR FREE....AGGGGAAINNN.....This enter straight in my Olympus of "Value for Money Chart" with a LEGENDARY 10/10!

visual update no one asked for that breaks all mods, doesnt really change a lot visually, and makes it so now you have to have an internet connection at all times for a single player story driven survival game? trash.

Exploring the world and raiding dungeons and owning slaves is a lot of fun. Any time I play any other survival open world game I have a hard time not dropping it and coming back to Conan. It has that secret ingredient (owning slaves).

Jank is something I can put up with but paywalling basic QOL mechanics (sorting chests) ,even when I own all the DLCs, is one of the slimiest moves I've ever seen. I'llchange my review if they fix this.
I love the character design, building mechanics, and the combat is super fun! Lots of exploring and fighting to do in this game but base building is also very in depth.

Can't connect to steam online services whenever I try to join a server. Works perfectly fine until the game relaunches to join a server. Funcom, fix your game. This has been an issue for years. Every other game works perfectly with Steam. Steam is working fine. It's your game, I bought it, can't play it.

I've had a lot of fun. I think the setting, enemy variety, map design, and overall mechanics come together to make a game that doesn't have any competition in terms of a large scale fantasy survival RPGs.

But a lot of the game is filled with content where if the devs did small tweaks it'd be insanely better. I feel like an "enhanced" version should have had bug fixes, rebalancing, had more content added, and addressed the huge list of player requests over the years.

We got the "enhanced" edition, with environments that sorta look better but really just have fancy grass and shadows with a piss filter no matter how warm or cold the environment. Something about the lighting on characters and interiors looks flatter than they did before, too.

And the jank is just so unnecessary for a 2026 "enhanced" game. For example there is a large open curtain that's open on the right side but not one that's inverted to be open on the left side. I don't understand how that even got pushed through to the full game without a single person asking where the other one is. And being able to rotate them would be a totally acceptable answer but of course you can't. You can't place pillows at an angle to lean them on furniture. If you have a standing torch near a wall and want to make changes to the surrounding structures, you'll have to take down each torch because the hitbox on them is way thicker and conflicts with base building. Placing walls on any structure that's more than one story pretty much always results in placing walls on the other side of whatever you're pointing at at some point because the targeting ignores walls arbitrarily. Oh and I almost forgot to mention to place armor displays you either have to have played the game in 2023 to unlock them in the battle pass or spend more money in the randomly rotating cash shop on a game whose complete edition is $150 when not on sale. I just can never understand how that stuff flies.

I'm at almost 50 hours on this playthrough and I plan to clock more hours on the enhanced release but the only reason I can continue is because my friends make the game fun.

Easily the best sandbox survival game i've played. As the title suggests the game's visuals were enhanced DRAMATICALLY from the previous version, so much so that I was almost expecting the game to be a laggy un-optimized mess. Never been more happy to be wrong. I almost forgot to mention the graphics overhaul was FREE. Many other titles including ARK charged the price of an entirely NEW GAME when they finally figured out how to revamp their visuals!! Love the way they're operating, bought some DLC purely out of support for that move. Alright enough glazing here's what I have to say with less than 20 hours in the game so far... 

Will EASILY put hundreds of hours into this game. Yea i'm not done glazing. I'm personally not a fan of building up a nice base just for it to get destroyed by unemployed bums who seem to have more time on their hands than humanly possible so I really only play PVE and sometimes co-op when it comes to survival games. Problem is I usually end up getting bored with the lack of things to do as i'm sure most do with the sandbox/survival genre but you won't have to worry about that with conan exiles. If you're more of a builder the building mechanics are very smooth and engaging even for people who don't often build such as myself. The amount of detail that goes into the decorations and even foundational pieces are unreal (no pun intended) and the scenery of the map can leave you wanting to explore simply just to go sight seeing. Not that there isn't already enough reason to explore the map as you can find dungeons, caves, camp sites to enslave thralls (the npcs that are mostly hostile with great ai) and convert them to fight or work for you...or simply rob them of everything and end their bloodline, and so much more. I never run out of reasons to wander outside the base. 

As I partially mentioned regarding the combat mechanics: The thralls specifically have great ai that differ depending on what they're equipped with, Keeps each fight engaging and less repetitive. I will be honest and say that the other creatures ai are slightly worse than the thralls and can get pretty repetitive but still offers a different experience and option if you get tired of raiding camps. As for your character There are a plethora of weapons to arm yourself with that have different animations and combinations depending on the class (There are so many weapon classes I don't remember how many there are) each one having their own play style. You get to pick a race that has their own unique religion that offers buffs and exclusive items that give you a reason to make make new characters. There's also a level up system that gradually boosts your stats and gives u options on niche bonuses that cater to different kinds of play styles giving even more reason to explore different characters/builds. Progression so far has been great.

Can't say too much on the story line/end game given my hours but so far i'm having a blast on finding lore by exploring the map. No issues on the gameplay or progression or graphics or variety of things to do so far. To summarize the whole review the combat/building/exploration are all a 10/10. I'm really trying to think of something negative to say to even this review out but I mean the reason I made the review in the first place is because of how positively the first impression of the game is so yea... If you like games such as ark and palworld and really any sandbox/survival game but end up getting bored sooner than later like myself then give this a shot while it's still on sale I can gaurantee you'll get your moneys worth. Keep an eye on my hours and if it doesn't double in a week's time you can take everything i said with a grain of salt. If it does, do yourself a favor and TRY THE GAME!!!

I love this game. I repurchased it on PC cause of Nostalgia when I herd that it was Enhanced! Do Not Buy this game if you want to Play on Official Server for the epic PvP experience 'cause every combat engagement is a Rubber Band Lag Fight. By the time I realized that combat was asinine it was to late to get a refund.

There is a jank which permeates this whole game. Conan Exiles is somehow both ahead and behind other survival games. If you can look past the jank there is quite a jem here, but, for people like me, it fails to come together enough for the game to shine through it's roughness.

Combat is at times fun. It is at it's best when involving multiple parties, such as leading a team to take on a whole camp, or stumbling upon a 3 way fight between bandits, crocodiles, and spiders. Most of the time it is rather trivial, for example a rhino is extremely easy to fight without even using any dodging. Just strafe sideways at a slight jog to avoid all it's charges. Crocodiles are also easy to evade as they only spawn near water and cannot swim. No I'm not joking, if you dip your toes in water, crocs will give up as they are somehow a land-only crocodile afraid of water.

There are many upsides to Conan Exiles, for I love love love the climbing. It really changes how one approaches the map and navigation. I enjoy taking thralls, but wish they had more layers of usefulness. As it stands, a thrall does one thing, which is quite literally in it's name. Your crafting tables don't actually need to be manned by someone, so you only need a thrall for crafting speed. So many of my crafting tables are empty as I feel I don't need "Cimmeran Smelter II" to smelt my ore faster.

There does seem to be lore and story to be found here, requiring much exploring. I quite like this, the environmental story telling is quite good, if sparse. The few NPCs which you can talk to on the other hand come off as quite dry, which is strange because I feel that sometimes the game embraces the camp inherent in Conan, such as pirate themed bandits using bad pirate accents and silly voice lines, for example "Me and my mates are as thick as thieves!", but NPCs such as Conan himself are just there I guess, and have little to say. Conan himself is rather a let down, any comments you see calling him Conan from Wish are quite true.

In the end, Conan Exiles, while having many novel and interesting ideas about survival games, fails to reach the heights other games do, while having many individual parts which are indeed greater then other games. Some games are greater then the sum of it's parts, Conan just might be less then the sum of it's parts. I might come back to Conan and try to find a gem underneath, but right now the game just fails to call me back.

P.S. There is an absolutely hilarious Easter Egg in the game involving those guys from Battlefield Friends, it is the best voice acting and most distinct personalities in the entire game and if the game had more of this within it, it would have been enough to bring me back.
Good survival game so far. Nudity setting seems pointless as there is no sex in it at all but running across a half naked woman from time to time is a nice perk, I guess.

Don't pay full price for anything by these people.

  Even if you're not concerned about the price; you have to wait for a TIMED ROTATION SYSTEM to have a chance at purchasing any content you may want.  As if this stuff is exciting to wait for.   
 This is another company siphoning funds for mediocre assets. (just like most gaming industries now.)
 Greedy ♥♥♥♥♥ doing greedy ♥♥♥♥ things and people lap it up. I hate this criminal paywall ♥♥♥♥. Anyway.

  The game is fun on creator mode, building bases is fun, the thrall system is clunky but entertaining,
  The Enhanced edition has been fun, looks great, takes up much less space than before (my crying ssd thanks you) and has all the usual bugs these kinds of games come with:

  Movement in creator mode is buggy (Sometimes you move at a full sprint, other times you move at crawl speed for no discernable reason to Me), 
  Movement of thralls is buggy (Sometimes they stay where you tell them to, most of the time they walk away and do their own thing), 
  Lots of pieces don't play well together (foundation design is key if you want to build something other than a big square.) ,
  Lots of clipping as seen in most Unreal Engines (Your character or Thralls infinite walking in place\cant move at all.)  
  Lots of game restarts.
  Lots of available servers to choose from without passwords\without monthly subscriptions (generous community members)

I love this game, so definitely understand that my “review” comes with a little bias. 😄 I’ve owned Conan since the day it launched on Xbox and probably have close to 700 hours in it there alone.

First thing: the Enhanced version.
This is one of those rare gamer/studio wins where everybody actually comes out happy. Funcom absolutely nailed this update, and I really hope it eventually makes its way to console. The game looks better, runs better, and honestly feels like something I could sink another 1,000 hours into without even realizing it.

The modding scene is also starting to get updated for Enhanced, so things are looking really good on that front too. And combining the two main maps together? Absolutely incredible. The fact I no longer need to run two separate servers just to play both maps feels like a gift from Crom himself.

At its core, Conan is a true open-world survival sandbox… with actual sand this time. It mixes survival gameplay with RPG and story elements, great boss fights, and some genuinely awesome places to explore. One of my favorite parts has always been recruiting thralls and animals to build up your own army.

The gameplay loop is basically:
Explore world → find something horrifying that stomps your face into the dirt → come back later stronger and get revenge.

There are giant camps, cities, caves, and dungeons to explore, tons of hidden easter eggs, and a surprisingly solid journey/quest system to help guide new players along. And if you’re into building, Conan has some incredible DLC building sets that let people create insane castles, kingdoms, and masterpieces…

…or, if you’re like me, slightly crooked squares that somehow still count as a base. 😄

Pros:
- Beautiful world and building blocks
- Fun gameplay
- Lots to craft and build
- Large pool of mods and workshop enhancements
- Active RP community
- Siege battles! (pvp)
Cons:
- Dumb AI (Behavior and pathing)
- Extremely limited and shallow PVE content
- No main story or any story. Just scatterted bits of lore.
- Bugs! Nothing game breaking but you'll find goofy things occasionally. 
- Goofy PVP mechanics. Specially if you factor in the lag. 
- Tethering system in co-op (Why devs? why...)

Overal it's a fun game if you have a bunch of friends to play with and pvp. 
Solo play can be fun too with base building and exploring. 
The game also provides a lot of tools, specially with added mods to create setups for roleplay.

I know I'm late to the party, only been playing close to a month now and it's honestly the most fun I've had in these types of games, a certain charm that doesn't come in other games, like rewarding exploration, deep dungeons, active NPCs, and mounts and tamable animals and thralls, world bosses and events, soooooo many different styles of crafting and building based off the different factions and races in the game allowing for Soo much diversity it's great, and all in conjunction with an RPG system I really enjoy.
It's been hard to put this game down, really hope future me see's this and is still playing.
To my friends who see this please get this and play with me! 😁😁

This game can be summed up in one word: mediocre. Conan Exiles definitely isn't a bad game, but it’s not great either. I should mention I'm playing the Enhanced Edition, so I have no idea what it was like before. If you want to run around a huge world solo or with friends, slay some monsters, and build a base, this game is for you. You can host your own PvE server and fine-tune the balance and gameplay settings, and that level of customization is a huge plus.

Every aspect of the game generally works, but once you dig deeper, you notice flaws that are technically forgivable. Yet there’s always this nagging feeling of incompleteness, like you’re playing a beta and the devs just need to polish it a bit before the full release... except the game is actually 8 years old! It bums me out that a decent game feels so neglected by the developers, as if they just don't want to add those final touches.

That's why my review is leaning neutral, as I can't honestly recommend buying it. I'll list a few examples that felt questionable to me, and you can decide for yourself if the game is worth your time and money.

For instance, the inventory is way too old-school. There’s no indicator for food expiration or gear durability; you only see that info in the description after clicking the item icon. You can drag a sword or a pickaxe to the hotbar to swap items, but dragging them back from the bar to the inventory is buggy: the tool doesn't swap, it either moves to a slot without replacing anything, throws a "no slots available" error, or ends up in a loot bag at your feet (even though I definitely let go of the mouse over the inventory). Repairing armor and weapons also requires a bunch of item gymnastics. This isn't a micromanagement mechanic, it’s just a lack of QoL.

Then there's the world. This part of Hyboria consists of two massive maps, and it's a beautiful world with various biomes and climates. All the dungeons are unique with different entry requirements. However, there’s a massive lack of loot locations on the map; the frontier is very sparse on structures, and even existing ancient ruins are often empty. This clashes with the narrative: your character was exiled here specifically because the area is supposed to be packed with valuable artifacts from millennia of Hyborian history that even treasure hunters avoid. Unique loot mostly drops from bosses, while trash mobs drop almost nothing. There’s also some weird imbalance because after 10 hours of play, I randomly found a star metal tool in a plain chest at a low-tier bandit camp.

The game's balance is hit-or-miss. On one hand, the combat system based on stamina management feels responsive and mostly solid. But the mob AI is extremely primitive. This is especially obvious with mini-bosses (the ones with skull icons on their health bars) because they constantly get stuck since they just charge in a straight line and spam melee attacks once they close in. The problem is they’re often huge and get wedged in the terrain. You just stand there watching this monster's pathetic attempts to squeeze between two rocks and wonder if you should play along and move into melee range or just ignore the immersion, pull out a bow, and cheese it from a distance.

Building has its flaws too. The building system itself is cool; it accounts for structural integrity, so you can't build physically impossible giant halls and actually have to use your brain. However, there’s no basic thing like support columns for ceilings (or they're hidden somewhere deep in the menus). I’ve also seen reports that paid cosmetic DLC items like doors have been broken and unpatched for years.

The NPCs here are weird. First off, you can take one companion with you and set their behavior. It’s not very intuitive, and sometimes the follower just refuses to fight and gets mauled by mobs. Second, there’s one small city per map with neutral NPCs and traders. You have to go in alone because these NPCs aggro on your follower. The traders feel like they’re just there for show since their stock is usually just a single item that you don’t really need anyway.

I could go on listing these quirks for a long time. These flaws don’t totally ruin the gameplay, but after 8 years, you’d think they could have at least fixed how the inventory behaves.

Not sure how this compares to the original version, but the enhanced version is impressive for as old a game as it is. There are obvious signs of age, but also a lot of features showing the time it's been developed. If you like survival crafters, this is a solid one.

I will say, the videos and screenshots for this game don't do the game justice. It is gorgeous and quite immersive. And since I'm such a sucker for gorgeous, immersive games, not only did I buy this game for 40 bucks, I also decided to play the fool and basically buy all the cosmetics from the shop. Yep, I spent [i]that[/i] much money total. It was worth it for me though. Not only do I really enjoy beautiful games that draw me in, I like to reward developers with money and my attention playing those games when they give me what I like, as it should be. 

I never really played this game before the enhanced edition. It seemed fun, I just had other things going on that were fun too. I'm glad I got to try it now though. I highly recommend this game to anyone who is a sucker for graphical eye-candy like I am. Plus, adult content.

[b]P.S.[/b], 

If you're into playing games like these offline, you can do that, you just have to do a bit of a work-around to get it going. [b]Here's one that worked for me:[/b] 

Right-click Conan Exiles in your Steam Library → Properties → General → Launch Options
Paste the following (adjust the path to wherever your game is installed):

"D:\SteamLibrary\steamapps\common\Conan Exiles\ConanSandbox\Binaries\Win64\ConanSandbox-Win64-Shipping.exe" %command%

To find your actual install path: right-click the game → Properties → Installed Files → Browse, then click the address bar to copy it.

[b]Important:[/b] If your install path has spaces in it (e.g., "Conan Exiles"), make sure you're using straight quotes " and not curly/smart quotes. Copying from a browser or forum can sometimes introduce smart quotes, which Windows won't recognize. If you get a "Windows cannot find..." error, retype the quotes manually in the Launch Options field.

[b]Note:[/b] This method means you won't be able to join any online or BattlEye-protected servers, but since you're playing single-player offline, that shouldn't matter. Hope this helps. Also note that if you wish to revert the game to the original state, all you need to do is delete the command line from the Launch Options section.

[h1]I became exiled...[/h1]
I never played the original game before. Only by chance I slipped into this relaunch because of a Youtube video about the graphical changes and because they cut the price for the complete edition by two thirds. Did not expect anything, found pure gold.

Honestly it easily is a 10/10 game on a good day. The devs did everything so right, that other competitors such as Pax Dei or Nightingale wished they would have done even small parts of. Now those two lay dead in the dust, while the barbarian kings emerge victorious once more. Wasn't even a close fight.

Either way, I got completely sucked into Conan Exiles, I'm having a meaningful learning, crafting, base building and multiplayer experience. The graphics look wonderful for environments and okay-ish for character models.  And despite UE5 and having a large open world, client side performance is flawless. The game somehow consumes way less system resources than mentioned competitors while looking better. 

The only bad thing so far was the server performance for multiplayer. Everyone is rubbanding like crazy and hits don't register a lot of times. Every fight becomes a roll with the dice and it's a mood killer for a lot of people. Funcom did not prepare adequately for the resurgence of players.

[b][u]Final Verdict:[/u][/b]
Singeplayer offline mode 10/10 
Multiplayer online mode 8/10*

*[spoiler]Will be 11/10 once the servers run properly.[/spoiler]

Look man, I like breaking rocks, cutting trees and domesticating animals. I am not a gamer and am not a fan of slaughter, tons of blood, horror, or cut scenes. I am a simple gal. I like explorin' and only seek to kill bad guys if they are in my way of explorin', breakin' rock, or cuttin' trees. My husband likes building shelters so we make a good team. I am not a fan of makin' slaves, but that is a moral thing. Fun game for someone that only really like Skyrim-type play.

I'm having issues with connection and desync. But they are constantly updating to try fix things up.
The only problem is that i have to constantly make space on my C:\ to update every now and then.
I also have issues with fps on a high end amd PC.

all the graphical enhancements in the world won't change how bad this game feels to play. clunky and laggy doesn't begin to describe it. servers are complete trash, and you will LOSE ALL YOUR GEAR IF YOU CRASH and DIE. restarted new characters THREE TIMES because of SERVER LAG and just lost all my gear due to this bug. i'll never buy another Funcom game.

great update, appreciate them giving it to those who had the original game for free. but the official servers are a laggy mess and I cannot recommend this game for what should be its best part being its biggest issue
They didn't ask if they should, only if they could. "Enhanced" is a poor way to put increased requirements for the same game people had for almost a decade.

Already one of my favorite games of all time and imo Top 3 survival games ever made. This update just pushed it into an entirely different realm. 

PVP is solid, Building is incredible, enough fun pve content to keep you engaged when you're not raiding. All in all a nearly perfect survival game. Runs very well on my mid level laptop.

The game it's good but FUNCOM wtf it's wrong with y'all servers , I came back to the new enchanted version , the servers are 3x worst than before , a good server has 200 ping , bad ones +500 , you want to keep and bring new people to a game that bearly runs , where you fight  an enamy , dogde and the server pull you back into the attack , what PVP, what RAIDS , just be happy if you can cut a tree, for ♥♥♥♥ sake , yall really wanna kill the game , just change the parteners for server hosting or at least implement a limit to stop the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lag and latency .
I play single player and love it. I can setup server rules to match how I want to play so I can enjoy the game my way. Love the building and thrall capturing system. Combat is simple and fun.
horrible laggy servers for almost 10 years. npc animals walk through your base. unplayable unless you are solo. the official servers are so bad 500+ ping even most community servers are ♥♥♥♥♥♥
One of the best, most in-depth, and content rich survival games I've ever played. Best enjoyed in PVE.

Putting QoL sorting chest behind real money microtransactions is an absolutely no no for me. Putting FunCon on "ignore" and won't ever buy new games from them again. Considering I really love this game, having lost all interest in it because of that should say it all. 

Sell cosmetics if you need to, sell good (and a good amount of) extra quality content via dlc. But do not make consumers pay for QoL stuff, bug fixes etc.

Not to mention all the bugs and problems including vanishing spectors in the northern area, getting stuck on entering the path/tunnel between northern area and swamp, thralls vanishing in hills etc. Also lots of bugs that have been around in the old version too.

I'm done with this. Thanks for ruining it FunCon.

Edit: I may change this review if I get new information that changes things.

I can't really recommend this game to anyone. I have over 1200hrs on Conan Exiles, and in that time I've encountered so many infuriating bugs (the most egregious of which caused you to fall through floors if too many people were online). 

Decided to jump back into to it with the UE5 update, and I can see not much has changed.

Visually speaking, this game absolutely cooks. But there is SO much ping right now if you're not constantly resetting your servers that it's unplayable. Enemies no where near me will strike me down or teleport around like a flash-stepping Naruto main character that you can rarely land a hit. 

Even when the ping is closer to tolerable the enemy AI still has a single brain cell it has to share with the entire server, so enemies (especially animals) often just sit there and take it. I got a Cimmerian Berzerker for free cause he didn't seem to mind that I was mollywomping him into an intern.

It's just so frustrating, I love this game, I want to love playing it. But the game itself seems to want me to hate it instead, and the Devs apparently don't care one way or another. Glaring issues that make playing the game intolerable? Guess it's time to release some more outrageously priced cosmetics.

While I don't mind spending money to support my favorite games, the lowest amount of premium currency is $10, there's no $1 or $5 options and no way to get them outside of purchasing. Just a SINGLE Crom coin away from that cosmetic you really want? Too bad, screw you, shell out $10 for your shelf.

The game was already really fun but this graphics update makes the world look and feel alive more than ever. Lots of content to do in the base map, which are mostly grim but you might find base building is really cosy with barkeeper thralls, dancers and your patrons. Its a true sandbox and I can wholeheartedly recommend this game to anyone likes survival rpg's or base building games. You can be a true barbarian with tons of slaves and gritty bases, or you can throw Roman parties every night. Its trully your choice.

But be aware of a big problem that probably wont get fixed soon. Online play will be horribly laggy if you play in a big server. Which is a huge shame because big servers feel very alive and fits the worldbuilding intended. I still recommend the game but in crowded online play sadly you'll have a bad time for the foreserable future.

What I like about this game, is the scale and the atmosphere. Physically the game has so much to explore. Visually, I feel present in the world of Conan Exiles. There's a lot to do, to farm, to kill, to build, to learn and craft, to tame, to kidnap and turn into thralls. For a building survival crafter - this game easily keeps me interested whether it be solo or with friends. While early on access into content can come somewhat easily, MASTERY is a long path. I think back to my initial fights with NPCs and beasts, my approach has adapted greatly over weeks of play. For such an old game, the developers ticked a lot of boxes to make the game have a low rate of feeling "boring" anytime soon. Technically, I've had no dramas playing this game, no bugs, no crashes. And gameplay itself is quite entertaining - if not lacking in a bit more polish - although what this would take even has me scratching my chin thinking over it.

The atmosphere and environments are amazing, but the gameplay is pretty bad :

- A rigid and clunky target-lock combat system.

- Poor audio mixing, some sounds are much too loud.

- Impossible balancing because the game mixes PvP and PvE together.

- A lot of modern quality-of-life features are missing.

- A constant need to check the wiki because so many things are poorly explained.

- An outdated and limited building system (for example, there are no half-floor).

- Bugs, tons of bugs, a whole legion of bugs,  mechanics not working properly... a nightmare.

- Terrible optimization. Stuttering everywhere.

- One last thing worth mentioning because it says a lot about how polished the game is : there are no underwater creatures at all, and no NPCs can go into the water...


In short, this game is very enjoyable on the surface, but once you reach the mid-game (around level 30), its flaws start jumping at your throat and suffocating your desire to keep playing.

 This game could be great with a massive amount of polishing and refinement. In its current state, it feels more like an early access game than a finished one.

Got game at very begining, enjoying my self since. Conan, the legend, live, love burn with life , slay and survive.

Over the years they added magic to game as well
You can solo, co-op or servers, your choice

enjoying U5 upgrade

Got the game on Steam but apparently I have no way to download the DevKit without installing the stupid Epic launcher...

Still some fun stuff in the game. I just hate companies pushing their apps and garbage like that down our throats.
The enhance version looks really nice compared to the old one. That lead me to give it another chance, after the original just had 4h played. For now, it feels nice.

Solid game, but not playable if you just want to have a game with a few friends rather than play on an official MMO-esque server. The "co-op" mode is a weird beast where everyone is "tethered" to the host, meaning they can't be very far from the host, ever. If the host dies, everyone teleports back to the host. If anyone ever wanders too far or the host goes in a different direction, everyone then starts automatically running wildly towards the host. This is unplayable for an open-world game. If the developers ever add an actual co-op mode where I don't have to go through the mess of setting up a dedicated server (I haven't had to do that in years), I'll change the review to positive. And yes, the developers did make a supposedly simpler "dedicated server launcher" and it still requires all the mess of port forwarding and everything else. If you have an ISP that doesn't allow for that (or the many other requirements for a dedicated server), again, you're out of luck. Awful design.

tried to play oceania pvp servers, however every night since release, during raid hours ping spikes and sits around 300-400ms, at this latency the game does not let you log in to your chosen server so you are unable to play at all or defend from those that are still connected. funcom i have this game on console also, clocking over 2500hrs, i bought it again on steam especially for launch, this is a major problem for your player base and needs immediate attention, will not recommend to anyone to start official at this stage.

Unfortunately, as much as I want to, I cannot recommend this game... and that downvote isn't even based on anything having to do with the game itself.

It's the players.

Much like Arc Raiders, Rust, DayZ, or Escape From Tarkov, when you die (unless you play on a pve server with the very specific server option checked) you lose everything.  The other player has the ability, and usually the mentality, to take it all and leave you starting from scratch. 

And much like those other games, they don't care if you're a brand new player just barely getting started.
That's not even covering the cheater problem.  People making bases that float in the sky with no part of that base touching the ground, displaying unlimited stamina, and taking no fall damage.  Using x-ray mods to figure out where they have to bomb on your base to avoid all of your pancake and honeycomb layers.  Duping and exploiting.  It's all bad.

In 1000 hours I have NEVER been on a pvp server that was cheater free.  Not once.  I have NEVER been on a server with base raiding enabled that didn't have end-game players looking for sandstone starter bases to raid... even though the poor guy had only been on that server for an hour and was just getting started.

The best part?  90% of the servers are under 20 population.  That's right, tiny population servers, and this is how the community treats one another.  Take it from the guy with 1k hours on record on just his Steam account alone, this game is great for the short solo fun you can have on single player "servers"... but on multiplayer, the servers all lag horribly and the "community" is terrible.

Rant over.
one of the best survival games out there if you can control yourself and not skip too much of the content
Best survival game on the market.  Private server is the way to go though.

Conan Exiles is one of the most enjoyable survival games I’ve played. The world feels dangerous and immersive, and there’s always something to do, whether it’s exploring, fighting enemies, gathering resources, or building a massive base. The combat system is surprisingly fun, with different weapons and fighting styles that make battles feel intense.

One of the best parts of the game is the building system. You can create anything from small buildings to huge castles, and it’s easy to spend hours designing and improving your base. Playing with friends makes the experience even better, additionally thralls add such a unique part to the game it really makes it feel fleshed out to a certain degree.

The game does have some lag and performance issues at times, especially on busy servers, but despite that, it’s still a great survival experience overall. If you enjoy open-world survival games with good combat and creative building, Conan Exiles is definitely worth trying.
This game just feels so empty. Instead of progression, the game just makes you do a bunch of side quests. I also wish the combat would be a little smoother. It feels more stressful than it is fun.
Played a lot between 2021–2023 then took a break. The UE5 update brought me back, it’s one of the best updates this game has had in a long time. Much needed and very well done!
I forgot to review this. It is good, better than it has a right to be. Get it, live it, love it.
Best survival game I have ever played. Really captures the feel of the Conan universe.

They did a fantastic job with the UE5 upgrade, the game looks better than ever and still runs great. What I love the most is that the 2 maps are now merged on a single server, I almost feel like I'm playing Conan for the first time again and for free! 

Steam Deck performance is great with some lossless scaling applied. I kept the nanite and lumen settings low, uncapped the frame rate, adjusted lossless scaling to mailbox settings and 2x frame gen. The rest of settings you can play around with to your liking. 

The only negative I have is the ping situation, it needs to be raised to over 400 or removed. I had to play on a new server, because my main server always has high ping during pvp time. 

Major thanks to Funcom for this free update, I hope this is a new beginning for Conan, with new map expansions in the future!

I never was into Conan before. I played almost 700 hours of Ark Survivor: Evolved and was my favorite game. After this visuals update, the game became much more fluid, much more streamlined. Not just in graphic visual, but in game mechanics. It is becoming my go-to game after Ark.

Quality of life items locked being the store page.  paid auto sort chests.  Nah, I'm good Funcom.  I didn't pay for the game to then be nickel and dimed for QoL stuff.

Edit:  When and if this changes I will change my review.

The game was great even on day of release. This is the type of game that I played being on my friend's party, and decided that moment, that I will buy it. Few weeks ago, the game got Enchanced Edition. I thought, that my NVidia 1660 6Gb will have problems with running it, but it run great on low settings and loading GPU only on 60%, while having medium settings run on 48-60 fps. The game on UE5 with low settings looks as the Legacy version on High settings. The latest weeks the nVidia drivers had additional optimization for the game.

Another thing is that they changed loot system. The game become more easy and casual. If previously I had to move to Snow Boome for the Black Ice and Star Metal, then now I can get it as a loot from the undead in Dead City, or killing event enemies. There are many changes in comparison with the game from 6 years ago.

I would definitely suggest any players who is looking for a survival game in an big open world. The game can be completed by killing bosses, looting their artefacts, combining them into a key, that will release you from these Exiled lands, so you have some kind of a "mission".

Wow... I tried this game to see how it looks in virtual reality using the UEVR mod. I was VERY pleasantly surprised. It's like Valheim modded for VR but on steroids. With the gigantic open world feel of Zelda Breath of the Wild. The character models look like they're on steroids too... And the female entertainers are... definitely entertaining in VR. O.O I definitely chose a good time to jump in after the unreal 5 engine upgrade... 

To get the mod working you cannot play online with Battleye or you will probably get banned by it. You must disable Battleye in the game launch menu and then play in either single player or local coop. I've only tried single player so far.

Then to get the UEVR mod working properly I had to override propagate alpha. Before doing this it didn't look right. It should be played in native stereo (not synced sequential) for the best results, and this is how to achieve that. Check discord for info on how to do this. I am so glad I just dove right in and got all the dlc in a bundle. What a great deal. I appreciate all the armor sets so much more in VR. 

I'm finding all sorts of taxidermy loot of monster heads and full figurines that is getting me super hyped to explore the game.

Unofficial Servers are the way to go with this game. Game got a free UE5 update. Very low lag. Just enjoy the scenery again and enjoy some building. There is more content now then there was years ago. And get this, you can travel between the Exiled Lands map and Isle of Siptah now. If you have a sorcery teleporter you can travel to that portal on the other map. Also, the benches now pull from nearby boxes. And you can enable a compass under the UI option. This game has gotten some good improvement. Solid 8+/10.

incredible splendid graphic. Fun to play. There is an issue with lag if you put down the biggest stations but otherwise it's fine. Due to bad vision I dearly hope that the devs insert a UI scale option in the settings (like VEIN has). It would really help a lot.
It is prettier now, same stuff after 8 years, add some content or give the game to anyone else to do something with.

The lag in this game is unreal.

34 ping, yet you swing at enemies and it doesn't register.

When i started playing this morning was 4 people and 40 ping for server, it seems ok. Fast Forward to couple hours later and the game is unplayable. You lose health from something you aren't near hitting you, running away does nothing and you die before the lag stops. This official servers are really bad.

This game had some Jankiness to it but worked fine before the UE5 update, but much like with many UE5 games it is now constantly stutters, drops frames and hangs. A weird note is now its useless to go out at night, as Torches do basically nothing. Maybe a lighting issue, maybe it's just me since I haven't played for a while. 
I Could have Recommended this game before the UE5 Update, but would warn of some Jankiness. Now there is no chance I'd recommend it. Steer Clear if you are Below 40xx Graphics Cards or Higher, and even then, you will probably still have these issues because it's an Engine Issue not a Game Issue.

I put in 300 hours in the past and then the servers ping became too high. It was always the best ping for that server. I wasnt able to play on my server, so I quit. Now, Im back at another 100hours and the same exact thing happened. Its the second day in a row going from 44 to 856. Only 7 people on the server, so its not bogged down. Its an official server too. If I leave this time, Ill be done with all funcom games. I dont recommend coming back, these guys are still morons and dont care.

It looks nice, I never played it before the visual upgrade so I can't say what it was like before. Combat isn't great, I have had enemies land hits on me despite not being aimed towards me. I haven't gone into multiplayer, only tried solo, it's a pretty bland experience. No real story to focus on, and the base building aspect got old fast. It kept me mildly entertained for the almost 30 hours I have put into it, but I don't feel the urge to continue playing.
The Lag is so immense, it makes almost impossible to play. I used to love the game... now it's completely broken. Sad.

Hold up until they stop updating.
Sounds weird right? 
But the thing is the devs are kinda bad and don't test, so they push updates that break the game.
Also the game lacks a bunch of stuff that you need mods to fix it, but the updates break the mods, can't even play.

SO can't recommend until the devs stop updating the game which is weird, but honestly it will be better to have a stable version since modders will have to fix the mess they will leave behind anyways. It is a weird scenario when a company trying to update a product can make it a worst experience if you are trying to play it.

Took away the sand physX and water is worse. Moving through water has no effect now and sand is a ugly solid texture unlike real sand AKA before the "enhanced" version. And that is the most annoying thing with UE5 the lack of physical reactions when moving in the world, the lack of physics from sand which sand is a massive part of the game you'll be seeing a lot of it is now downgraded it makes the world feel static and plastic.

They strive to make it look pretty and "realistic" but have made the world feel faker and uglier. i'm tired of the need for the extremely good looking textures while taking away physical reactions in the world.

I mean i like it but honestly the mods are what make it a fun game. In the end Funcom always destroys their own products lmao. The opening cut scene is skippable but 70% of the time it's not. Funcom will actually change the textures of items that your purchase from the Bazaar after you purchase it.
Without the mods this game did NOT survive the passing of time... even with the cosmetic upgrades its a mid tier game at best

QOL is terrible, there is so much survival focus the game is not at all fun to play and an unnecessary time sink. you have to have farm/harvest thralls to even be viable, unbalance weapon damages etc etc, the list is long for not playing
Offical servers are near to unplayable due to huge lag and cheaters  during raidtime a majority of the servers has so much ping you cant even  connect

The recent switch to UE5 has made this game unplayable for me.  Whenever I get into a fight it crashes and of course that means you die in this game and lose all your loot.  The devs only care about selling add ons and glam packs.  They thought they could add a pretty coat of paint to the game instead of fixing bugs and all they did was add more bugs and make the game unplayable.
